question: How do you handle missing data in pandas: fillna, dropna, and interpolate?
answer:
Detecting nulls:
  df.isna().sum()           # null count per column
  df.isna().any(axis=1)     # rows with any null

dropna():
  df.dropna()                          # drop rows with any null
  df.dropna(subset=['id', 'revenue'])  # drop rows with nulls in specific columns
  df.dropna(thresh=5)                  # keep rows with at least 5 non-null values
  df.dropna(axis=1)                    # drop columns with any null

fillna():
  df['col'].fillna(0)                  # fill with constant
  df['col'].fillna(df['col'].mean())   # fill with column mean
  df['col'].fillna(method='ffill')     # forward fill (propagate last valid value)
  df['col'].fillna(method='bfill')     # backward fill
  df.fillna({'col1': 0, 'col2': 'unknown'})  # per-column fill values

interpolate() for numeric time series:
  df['price'].interpolate(method='linear')   # linear interpolation
  df['price'].interpolate(method='time')     # time-weighted (needs DatetimeIndex)

DE best practice: understand WHY values are null before filling. Filling revenue with 0 vs mean has very different business implications. Document null handling decisions in pipeline code.
